fact_data.management: Monitor for hypotension
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Monitor for hypotension when sildenafil is co-administered with amlodipine
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Hypotension

fact_data.management: Frequent monitoring of trough blood levels of tacrolimus is recommended and adjust the dose when appropriate
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Amlodipine may increase the systemic exposure of cyclosporine or tacrolimus when co-administered. Frequent monitoring of trough blood levels of cyclosporine and tacrolimus is recommended and adjust the dose when appropriate
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Amlodipine may increase the systemic exposure of tacrolimus

fact_data.management: Limit the dose of simvastatin in patients on amlodipine to 20 mg daily
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Co-administration of simvastatin with amlodipine increases the systemic exposure of simvastatin. Limit the dose of simvastatin in patients on amlodipine to 20 mg daily
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increases the systemic exposure of simvastatin

fact_data.management: Monitor for symptoms of hypotension and edema, may require dose reduction
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Co-administration with CYP3A inhibitors (moderate and strong) results in increased systemic exposure to amlodipine and may require dose reduction. Monitor for symptoms of hypotension and edema when amlodipine is co-administered with CYP3A inhibitors to determine the need for dose adjustment
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased systemic exposure to amlodipine

fact_data.management: Frequent monitoring of trough blood levels of cyclosporine is recommended and adjust the dose when appropriate
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Amlodipine may increase the systemic exposure of cyclosporine or tacrolimus when co-administered. Frequent monitoring of trough blood levels of cyclosporine and tacrolimus is recommended and adjust the dose when appropriate
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Amlodipine may increase the systemic exposure of cyclosporine

fact_data.management: Blood pressure should be closely monitored
fact_data.sourcePhrase: No information is available on the quantitative effects of CYP3A inducers on amlodipine. Blood pressure should be closely monitored when amlodipine is co-administered with CYP3A inducers
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Unknown quantitative effects on amlodipine

fact_data.sourcePhrase: arrhythmia (including ventricular tachycardia and atrial fibrillation)

fact_data.recommendation: Use with caution; monitor for adverse effects

fact_data.management: Exercise caution when prescribing
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Cases of myopathy, including rhabdomyolysis, have been reported with atorvastatin co-administered with colchicine, and caution should be exercised when prescribing atorvastatin with colchicine
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Cases of myopathy, including rhabdomyolysis

fact_data.management: Not specified
fact_data.sourcePhrase: The risk of myopathy during treatment with statins is increased with concurrent administration of lipid-modifying doses of niacin
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased risk of myopathy

fact_data.management: Avoid co-administration
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Atorvastatin AUC was significantly increased with concomitant administration of atorvastatin 10 mg and cyclosporine 5.2 mg/kg/day compared to that of atorvastatin alone. The co-administration of atorvastatin with cyclosporine should be avoided
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Significantly increased atorvastatin AUC and bioavailability

fact_data.management: Caution when atorvastatin dose exceeds 20 mg
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Atorvastatin AUC was significantly increased with concomitant administration of atorvastatin 80 mg with clarithromycin (500 mg twice daily) compared to that of atorvastatin alone. Therefore, in patients taking clarithromycin, caution should be used when the atorvastatin dose exceeds 20 mg
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Significantly increased atorvastatin AUC

fact_data.management: Administer with caution
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Because it is known that the risk of myopathy during treatment with HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors is increased with concurrent administration of other fibrates, atorvastatin should be administered with caution when used concomitantly with other fibrates
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased risk of myopathy

fact_data.management: Varies by specific inhibitor - avoid tipranavir plus ritonavir, dose limits for others
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Atorvastatin AUC was significantly increased with concomitant administration of atorvastatin with several combinations of HIV protease inhibitors
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Significantly increased atorvastatin AUC

fact_data.management: Monitor patients appropriately
fact_data.sourcePhrase: When multiple doses of atorvastatin and digoxin were co-administered, steady state plasma digoxin concentrations increased by approximately 20%. Patients taking digoxin should be monitored appropriately
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased steady state plasma digoxin concentrations by approximately 20%

fact_data.management: Avoid concomitant administration
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Due to an increased risk of myopathy/rhabdomyolysis when HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors are co-administered with gemfibrozil, concomitant administration of atorvastatin with gemfibrozil should be avoided
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased risk of myopathy/rhabdomyolysis

fact_data.management: Not specified
fact_data.sourcePhrase: The risk of myopathy during treatment with statins is increased with concurrent administration of fibric acid derivatives
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased risk of myopathy

fact_data.management: Simultaneous co-administration recommended
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Concomitant administration of atorvastatin with inducers of cytochrome P450 3A4 (e.g., efavirenz, rifampin) can lead to variable reductions in plasma concentrations of atorvastatin. Due to the dual interaction mechanism of rifampin, simultaneous co-administration of atorvastatin with rifampin is recommended
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Variable reductions in plasma concentrations of atorvastatin

fact_data.management: Not specified
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Concomitant administration of atorvastatin with inducers of cytochrome P450 3A4 (e.g., efavirenz, rifampin) can lead to variable reductions in plasma concentrations of atorvastatin
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Variable reductions in plasma concentrations of atorvastatin

fact_data.management: Consider reduction in atorvastatin dosage
fact_data.sourcePhrase: The risk of skeletal muscle effects may be enhanced when atorvastatin is used in combination with niacin; a reduction in atorvastatin dosage should be considered in this setting
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Enhanced risk of skeletal muscle effects

fact_data.management: Not specified
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Contains one or more components that inhibit CYP 3A4 and can increase plasma concentrations of atorvastatin, especially with excessive grapefruit juice consumption (>1.2 liters per day)
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased plasma concentrations of atorvastatin

fact_data.management: Caution when atorvastatin dose exceeds 20 mg
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Atorvastatin AUC was significantly increased with concomitant administration of atorvastatin 40 mg and itraconazole 200 mg. Therefore, in patients taking itraconazole, caution should be used when the atorvastatin dose exceeds 20 mg
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Significantly increased atorvastatin AUC

fact_data.management: Not specified
fact_data.sourcePhrase: The risk of myopathy during treatment with statins is increased with concurrent administration of strong CYP 3A4 inhibitors. Concomitant administration of atorvastatin with strong inhibitors of CYP 3A4 can lead to increases in plasma concentrations of atorvastatin
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased risk of myopathy and increased plasma concentrations of atorvastatin

fact_data.management: Avoid concomitant use
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Atorvastatin AUC was significantly increased with concomitant administration of atorvastatin with the hepatitis C protease inhibitor telaprevir. Therefore, in patients taking the hepatitis C protease inhibitor telaprevir, concomitant use of atorvastatin should be avoided
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Significantly increased atorvastatin AUC

fact_data.management: Consider when selecting oral contraceptive
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Co-administration of atorvastatin and an oral contraceptive increased AUC values for norethindrone and ethinyl estradiol. These increases should be considered when selecting an oral contraceptive for a woman taking atorvastatin
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased AUC values for norethindrone and ethinyl estradiol

fact_data.sourcePhrase: Diabetes was reported as an adverse reaction

fact_data.recommendation: Use with caution; monitor for adverse effects

fact_data.management: Not specified
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Interactions of carvedilol with potent inhibitors of CYP2D6 isoenzyme (such as quinidine, fluoxetine, paroxetine, and propafenone) have not been studied, but these drugs would be expected to increase blood levels of the R(+) enantiomer of carvedilol
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased blood levels of the R(+) enantiomer of carvedilol

fact_data.management: Particular care should be taken when used perioperatively
fact_data.sourcePhrase: If treatment with carvedilol is to be continued perioperatively, particular care should be taken when anesthetic agents that depress myocardial function, such as ether, cyclopropane, and trichloroethylene, are used
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Not specified

fact_data.management: Particular care should be taken when used perioperatively
fact_data.sourcePhrase: If treatment with carvedilol is to be continued perioperatively, particular care should be taken when anesthetic agents that depress myocardial function, such as ether, cyclopropane, and trichloroethylene, are used
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Not specified

fact_data.management: Particular care should be taken when used perioperatively
fact_data.sourcePhrase: If treatment with carvedilol is to be continued perioperatively, particular care should be taken when anesthetic agents that depress myocardial function, such as ether, cyclopropane, and trichloroethylene, are used
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Not specified

fact_data.management: Particular care should be taken when used perioperatively
fact_data.sourcePhrase: If treatment with carvedilol is to be continued perioperatively, particular care should be taken when anesthetic agents that depress myocardial function, such as ether, cyclopropane, and trichloroethylene, are used
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Not specified
fact_data.interactantName: Anesthetic agents that depress myocardial function
fact_data.precipitantDrug: Anesthetic agents that depress myocardial function

fact_data.management: Regular monitoring of blood glucose is recommended
fact_data.sourcePhrase: β-blocker may enhance the blood-sugar-reducing effect of insulin and oral hypoglycemics. Therefore, in patients taking insulin or oral hypoglycemics, regular monitoring of blood glucose is recommended
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Enhanced blood-sugar-reducing effect

fact_data.management: Monitor ECG and blood pressure
fact_data.sourcePhrase: As with other β-blocker, if carvedilol tablet is administered with calcium channel blockers of the verapamil or diltiazem type, it is recommended that ECG and blood pressure be monitored
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Not specified
fact_data.interactantName: Calcium channel blockers of the verapamil type
fact_data.precipitantDrug: Calcium channel blockers of the verapamil type

fact_data.management: Monitor ECG and blood pressure
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Conduction disturbance (rarely with hemodynamic compromise) has been observed when carvedilol tablet is coadministered with diltiazem. As with other β-blocker, if carvedilol tablet is administered with calcium channel blockers of the verapamil or diltiazem type, it is recommended that ECG and blood pressure be monitored
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Conduction disturbance (rarely with hemodynamic compromise)

fact_data.management: Patients should be observed for signs of bradycardia or heart block, particularly when one agent is added to pre-existing treatment with the other
fact_data.sourcePhrase: The concomitant administration of amiodarone or other CYP2C9 inhibitors such as fluconazole with carvedilol may enhance the β-blocking activity, resulting in further slowing of the heart rate or cardiac conduction. Patients should be observed for signs of bradycardia or heart block, particularly when one agent is added to pre-existing treatment with the other
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Enhanced β-blocking activity, further slowing of the heart rate or cardiac conduction

fact_data.management: Patients should be observed for signs of bradycardia or heart block, particularly when one agent is added to pre-existing treatment with the other
fact_data.sourcePhrase: The concomitant administration of amiodarone or other CYP2C9 inhibitors such as fluconazole with carvedilol may enhance the β-blocking activity, resulting in further slowing of the heart rate or cardiac conduction. Patients should be observed for signs of bradycardia or heart block, particularly when one agent is added to pre-existing treatment with the other
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Enhanced β-blocking activity, further slowing of the heart rate or cardiac conduction

fact_data.management: Patients should be observed for signs of bradycardia or heart block, particularly when one agent is added to pre-existing treatment with the other
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Amiodarone and its metabolite desethyl amiodarone, inhibitors of CYP2C9, and P-glycoprotein increased concentrations of the S(-)-enantiomer of carvedilol by at least 2 fold. The concomitant administration of amiodarone or other CYP2C9 inhibitors such as fluconazole with carvedilol may enhance the β-blocking activity, resulting in further slowing of the heart rate or cardiac conduction
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased concentrations of the S(-)-enantiomer of carvedilol by at least 2 fold, enhanced β-blocking activity, further slowing of the heart rate or cardiac conduction

fact_data.management: Not specified
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Cimetidine increased AUC by about 30% but caused no change in Cmax
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased AUC by about 30% but caused no change in Cmax

fact_data.management: Not specified
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Rifampin reduced plasma concentrations of carvedilol by about 70%
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Reduced plasma concentrations of carvedilol by about 70%

fact_data.management: Increased monitoring of digoxin is recommended when initiating, adjusting, or discontinuing carvedilol tablets
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Digoxin concentrations are increased by about 15% when digoxin and carvedilol are administered concomitantly. Therefore, increased monitoring of digoxin is recommended when initiating, adjusting, or discontinuing carvedilol tablets
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Digoxin concentrations are increased by about 15%

fact_data.management: Regular monitoring of blood glucose is recommended
fact_data.sourcePhrase: β-blocker may enhance the blood-sugar-reducing effect of insulin and oral hypoglycemics. Therefore, in patients taking insulin or oral hypoglycemics, regular monitoring of blood glucose is recommended
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Enhanced blood-sugar-reducing effect

fact_data.management: Monitor ECG and blood pressure
fact_data.sourcePhrase: As with other β-blocker, if carvedilol tablet is administered with calcium channel blockers of the verapamil or diltiazem type, it is recommended that ECG and blood pressure be monitored
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Not specified

fact_data.mechanism: Both slow atrioventricular conduction and decrease heart rate
fact_data.management: Not specified
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Both digitalis glycosides and β-blockers slow atrioventricular conduction and decrease heart rate. Concomitant use can increase the risk of bradycardia
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased risk of bradycardia

fact_data.management: Monitor cyclosporine concentrations closely after initiation of carvedilol therapy and adjust the dose of cyclosporine as appropriate
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Modest increases in mean trough cyclosporine concentrations were observed following initiation of carvedilol treatment in 21 renal transplant subjects suffering from chronic vascular rejection
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Modest increases in mean trough cyclosporine concentrations

fact_data.management: When concomitant treatment is to be terminated, the β-blocker should be discontinued first. Clonidine therapy can then be discontinued several days later by gradually decreasing the dosage
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Concomitant administration of clonidine with a β-blocker may cause hypotension and bradycardia. When concomitant treatment with a β-blocker and clonidine is to be terminated, the β-blocker should be discontinued first. Clonidine therapy can then be discontinued several days later by gradually decreasing the dosage
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Hypotension and bradycardia

fact_data.management: Observe closely for signs of hypotension and/or severe bradycardia
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Patients taking a β-blocker and a drug that can deplete catecholamines (e.g., reserpine and monoamine oxidase inhibitors) should be observed closely for signs of hypotension and/or severe bradycardia
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Signs of hypotension and/or severe bradycardia

fact_data.management: Observe closely for signs of hypotension and/or severe bradycardia
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Patients taking a β-blocker and a drug that can deplete catecholamines (e.g., reserpine and monoamine oxidase inhibitors) should be observed closely for signs of hypotension and/or severe bradycardia

fact_data.management: Avoid concomitant use
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Concomitant use of drugs that inhibit the activity of this enzyme results in reduced plasma concentrations of the active metabolite of clopidogrel and a reduction in platelet inhibition. Avoid concomitant use of drugs that inhibit CYP2C19
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Reduced plasma concentrations of the active metabolite of clopidogrel and a reduction in platelet inhibition

fact_data.sourcePhrase: Coadministration of Plavix and NSAIDs increases the risk of gastrointestinal bleeding
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increases the risk of gastrointestinal bleeding

fact_data.management: Avoid concomitant use
fact_data.sourcePhrase: The exposure to the active metabolite of clopidogrel was decreased by 46% (Day 1) and 42% (Day 5) when Plavix and omeprazole were administered together. Mean inhibition of platelet aggregation was diminished by 47% (24 hours) and 30% (Day 5)
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Decreased exposure to the active metabolite of clopidogrel by 46% (Day 1) and 42% (Day 5), diminished mean inhibition of platelet aggregation by 47% (24 hours) and 30% (Day 5)

fact_data.sourcePhrase: coadministration of Plavix with warfarin increases the risk of bleeding because of independent effects on hemostasis
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increases the risk of bleeding

fact_data.sourcePhrase: Gastrointestinal and retroperitoneal hemorrhage with fatal outcome

fact_data.management: Patients should be closely watched during initiation of thyroid replacement therapy
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Initiating thyroid replacement therapy may cause increases in insulin or oral hypoglycemic requirements. Patients receiving insulin or oral hypoglycemics should be closely watched during initiation of thyroid replacement therapy.
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increases in oral hypoglycemic requirements

fact_data.mechanism: Thyroid hormones increase catabolism of vitamin K-dependent clotting factors and impair compensatory increases in clotting factor synthesis
fact_data.management: Patients should be watched very closely when thyroid is started; reduction in anticoagulant dosage will likely be required
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Thyroid hormones appear to increase catabolism of vitamin K-dependent clotting factors. If oral anticoagulants are also being given, compensatory increases in clotting factor synthesis are impaired. Patients stabilized on oral anticoagulants who are found to require thyroid replacement therapy should be watched very closely when thyroid is started. If a patient is truly hypothyroid, it is likely that a reduction in anticoagulant dosage will be required.
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Enhanced anticoagulant effect requiring reduction in anticoagulant dosage

fact_data.mechanism: Estrogens tend to increase serum thyroxine-binding globulin (TBg), decreasing free levothyroxine
fact_data.management: Patients without a functioning thyroid gland may need to increase their thyroid dose
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Estrogens tend to increase serum thyroxine-binding globulin (TBg). In a patient with a nonfunctioning thyroid gland who is receiving thyroid replacement therapy, free levothyroxine may be decreased when estrogens are started thus increasing thyroid requirements. Therefore, patients without a functioning thyroid gland who are on thyroid replacement therapy may need to increase their thyroid dose if estrogens or estrogen-containing oral contraceptives are given.
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Decreased free levothyroxine, increasing thyroid requirements in patients without functioning thyroid gland

fact_data.mechanism: Estrogen-containing oral contraceptives tend to increase serum thyroxine-binding globulin (TBg), decreasing free levothyroxine
fact_data.management: Patients without a functioning thyroid gland may need to increase their thyroid dose
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Estrogens tend to increase serum thyroxine-binding globulin (TBg). In a patient with a nonfunctioning thyroid gland who is receiving thyroid replacement therapy, free levothyroxine may be decreased when estrogens are started thus increasing thyroid requirements. Therefore, patients without a functioning thyroid gland who are on thyroid replacement therapy may need to increase their thyroid dose if estrogens or estrogen-containing oral contraceptives are given.
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Decreased free levothyroxine, increasing thyroid requirements in patients without functioning thyroid gland

fact_data.mechanism: Binds both T4 and T3 in the intestine, thus impairing absorption of these thyroid hormones
fact_data.management: Four to five hours should elapse between administration of colestipol and thyroid hormones
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Cholestyramine or colestipol binds both T4 and T3 in the intestine, thus impairing absorption of these thyroid hormones. Therefore, four to five hours should elapse between administration of cholestyramine and thyroid hormones.
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Impaired absorption of thyroid hormones

fact_data.mechanism: Binds both T4 and T3 in the intestine, thus impairing absorption of these thyroid hormones
fact_data.management: Four to five hours should elapse between administration of cholestyramine and thyroid hormones
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Cholestyramine or colestipol binds both T4 and T3 in the intestine, thus impairing absorption of these thyroid hormones. Therefore, four to five hours should elapse between administration of cholestyramine and thyroid hormones.
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Impaired absorption of thyroid hormones

fact_data.management: Patients should be closely watched during initiation of thyroid replacement therapy
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Initiating thyroid replacement therapy may cause increases in insulin or oral hypoglycemic requirements. Patients receiving insulin or oral hypoglycemics should be closely watched during initiation of thyroid replacement therapy.
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increases in insulin requirements

fact_data.management: Use with caution and with frequent monitoring of serum potassium
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Use of lisinopril with potassium-sparing diuretics (e.g., spironolactone, eplerenone, triamterene, or amiloride), potassium supplements, or potassium-containing salt substitutes may lead to significant increases in serum potassium
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Significant increases in serum potassium

fact_data.management: Monitor serum lithium levels frequently
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Lithium toxicity has been reported in patients receiving lithium concomitantly with drugs which cause elimination of sodium, including ACE inhibitors
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Lithium toxicity

fact_data.management: Use with caution and with frequent monitoring of serum potassium
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Use of lisinopril with potassium-sparing diuretics (e.g., spironolactone, eplerenone, triamterene, or amiloride), potassium supplements, or potassium-containing salt substitutes may lead to significant increases in serum potassium
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Significant increases in serum potassium

fact_data.sourcePhrase: Patients receiving coadministration of ACE inhibitor and mTOR inhibitor (e.g., temsirolimus, sirolimus, everolimus) therapy may be at increased risk for angioedema
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased risk for angioedema

fact_data.sourcePhrase: Patients taking concomitant neprilysin inhibitors may be at increased risk for angioedema
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased risk for angioedema

fact_data.management: Monitor renal function periodically
fact_data.sourcePhrase: co-administration of NSAIDs, including selective COX-2 inhibitors, with ACE inhibitors, including lisinopril, may result in deterioration of renal function, including possible acute renal failure
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Deterioration of renal function, including possible acute renal failure

fact_data.management: Do not coadminister aliskiren with lisinopril in patients with diabetes. Avoid use in patients with renal impairment (GFR <60 ml/min)
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Do not coadminister aliskiren with lisinopril and hydrochlorothiazide tablets in patients with diabetes. Avoid use of aliskiren with PRINZIDE in patients with renal impairment (GFR <60 ml/min)
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased risk of hypotension, syncope, hyperkalemia, and changes in renal function

fact_data.management: Avoid combined use of RAS inhibitors. Monitor blood pressure, renal function, and electrolytes
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Dual blockade of the RAS with angiotensin receptor blockers, ACE inhibitors, or direct renin inhibitors (such as aliskiren) is associated with increased risk of hypotension, syncope, hyperkalemia, and changes in renal function
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased risk of hypotension, syncope, hyperkalemia, and changes in renal function

fact_data.management: Use with caution and with frequent monitoring of serum potassium
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Use of lisinopril with potassium-sparing diuretics (e.g., spironolactone, eplerenone, triamterene, or amiloride), potassium supplements, or potassium-containing salt substitutes may lead to significant increases in serum potassium
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Significant increases in serum potassium

fact_data.management: Avoid combined use of RAS inhibitors. Monitor blood pressure, renal function, and electrolytes
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Dual blockade of the RAS with angiotensin receptor blockers, ACE inhibitors, or direct renin inhibitors (such as aliskiren) is associated with increased risk of hypotension, syncope, hyperkalemia, and changes in renal function
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased risk of hypotension, syncope, hyperkalemia, and changes in renal function

fact_data.management: Discontinue diuretic or increase salt intake prior to lisinopril initiation, or start lisinopril at 5 mg daily with close medical supervision
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Patients on diuretics and especially those in whom diuretic therapy was recently instituted, may occasionally experience an excessive reduction of blood pressure after initiation of therapy with lisinopril
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Excessive reduction of blood pressure

fact_data.management: Monitor serum potassium in such patients
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Coadministration of losartan with other drugs that raise serum potassium levels may result in hyperkalemia. Monitor serum potassium in such patients.
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Hyperkalemia

fact_data.management: Do not coadminister aliskiren with losartan potassium in patients with diabetes. Avoid use of aliskiren with losartan potassium in patients with renal impairment (GFR <60 mL/min)
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Do not coadminister aliskiren with losartan potassium in patients with diabetes. Avoid use of aliskiren with losartan potassium in patients with renal impairment (GFR <60 mL/min).
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased risks of hypotension, syncope, hyperkalemia, and changes in renal function (including acute renal failure)

fact_data.management: Monitor serum lithium levels during concomitant use
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Increases in serum lithium concentrations and lithium toxicity have been reported during concomitant administration of lithium with angiotensin II receptor antagonists. Monitor serum lithium levels during concomitant use.
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increases in serum lithium concentrations and lithium toxicity

fact_data.management: In general, avoid combined use of RAS inhibitors. Closely monitor blood pressure, renal function, and electrolytes
fact_data.sourcePhrase: Dual blockade of the RAS with angiotensin receptor blockers, ACE inhibitors, or aliskiren is associated with increased risks of hypotension, syncope, hyperkalemia, and changes in renal function (including acute renal failure) compared to monotherapy
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Increased risks of hypotension, syncope, hyperkalemia, and changes in renal function (including acute renal failure)

fact_data.management: Monitor renal function periodically in patients receiving losartan and NSAID therapy
fact_data.sourcePhrase: coadministration of NSAIDs, including selective COX-2 inhibitors, with angiotensin II receptor antagonists (including losartan) may result in deterioration of renal function, including possible acute renal failure
fact_data.clinicalEffect: Deterioration of renal function, including possible acute renal failure

fact_data.recommendation: Use with caution; monitor for adverse effects
